forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system in the Tasman Sea continues to move east. A cold front and trough will move over SA on the weekend. Another cold front will move over the south early next week, followed by a high pressure system.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers, tending to rain at times with thunderstorms in the east, easing during the morning before redeveloping about central and northern districts during the afternoon. Some thunderstorms may become severe, particularly across the Pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, ahead of a west to southwesterly change in the west, reaching the Eyre Peninsula in the evening.
A patchy rain band about western and central districts is slowly moving into eastern districts. Isolated thunderstorms about the west, moving into central districts. Northeast to northwesterly winds.
Isolated showers about the agricultural areas and the far north. Possible thunderstorms in the east and north. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

Isolated showers about the far north. Showers redeveloping about the agricultural areas during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, tending west to northwesterly about the south during the day.

text.p: Isolated showers about the agricultural area, most likely about southern coasts. Maximum temperatures slightly below average. South to southwesterly winds fresh about southern coasts.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: 1 to 5 mm about the west, increasing to 5 to 15 mm east of around Coober Pedy, 15t o 25 mm about central districts and the northeast. Possible falls up to 30 to 60 mm with thunderstorms.
